pathways.definition: This high-capacity, constitutive, and dynamin-independent pathway involves the formation of uncoated tubulovesicular primary carriers (CLICs) that mature into specialized early endocytic compartments (GEECs) rich in glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored proteins.

associated_genes.name: CDC42
associated_genes.Definition: A small Rho GTPase that regulates actin polymerization and is essential for CLIC/GEEC vesicle formation, trafficking, and maturation.

associated_genes.name: GBF1
associated_genes.Definition: activates ARF1 to tune CDC42 cycling at PM

associated_genes.name: ARF1
associated_genes.Definition: A small GTPase that initiates CLIC formation by recruiting necessary components at the plasma membrane.

associated_genes.name: ARHGAP10
associated_genes.Definition: returns CDC42 to cycling state for carrier formation

associated_genes.name: ARHGAP26
associated_genes.Definition: RhoGAP/BAR/SH3 protein central to CLIC/GEEC tubules

associated_genes.name: ACTR2
associated_genes.Definition: ARP2: branched actin for curvature and vesicle formation

associated_genes.name: ACTR3
associated_genes.Definition: ARP3: branched actin for curvature and vesicle formation

associated_genes.name: BAIAP2
associated_genes.Definition: IRSp53: membrane/actin coupling in CIE families incl. CLIC/GEEC

associated_genes.name: GBF1
associated_genes.Definition: A guanine nucleotide exchange factor (GEF) that activates Arf1, thus initiating the cascade that maintains Cdc42 cycling.

associated_genes.name: ARHGAP10
associated_genes.Definition: A RhoGAP protein that inactivates Cdc42, returning it to a cycling state and balancing actin dynamics.

associated_genes.name: ARHGAP26
associated_genes.Definition: Multidomain protein with a BAR, SH3, and RhoGAP domain; colocalizes with CLIC/GEEC carriers and regulates Cdc42 activity and membrane curvature.

associated_genes.name: LGALS3
associated_genes.Definition: Galectin-3: An extracellular lectin that clusters glycosylated cargo and lipids, initiating membrane curvature required for CLIC formation.

associated_genes.name: PICK1
associated_genes.Definition: Protein Interacting with C Kinase 1, involved in Arp2/3 regulation and thereby affects actin dynamics required for CLIC/GEEC vesicle formation.

associated_genes.name: RAB5A
associated_genes.Definition: Small GTPase that regulates the fusion of GEECs with early sorting endosomes, affecting downstream trafficking but not initial internalization.

associated_genes.name: PI3KCA
associated_genes.Definition: Phosphoinositide 3-kinase facilitates membrane trafficking and maturation of endosomes; important for fusion of GEECs but not for carrier formation.

associated_genes.name: DNM1
associated_genes.Definition: Although not required for CLIC formation (which is dynamin-independent), it is involved in **membrane recycling from GEECs** in certain cell types.

associated_genes.name: VCL
associated_genes.Definition: Mechanotransducer protein that regulates membrane tension; modulates CLIC/GEEC activity under mechanical stress but is not a core component.

associated_genes.name: CD44
associated_genes.Definition: A glycosylated transmembrane protein and cargo of the pathway; it clusters via galectin-3 but its presence is not required to activate the pathway.

associated_genes.name: ITGA5
associated_genes.Definition: These receptors are cargo for CLIC/GEEC and may influence clustering and curvature, but are not required for pathway formation or vesicle scission.

associated_genes.name: ITGB1
associated_genes.Definition: These receptors are cargo for CLIC/GEEC and may influence clustering and curvature, but are not required for pathway formation or vesicle scission.

associated_genes.name: LGALS1
associated_genes.Definition: Galectin-1: Like galectin-3, it can assist in clustering glycosylated cargo, but its role is context-dependent and not essential in all cell types.

associated_genes.name: CTBP1
associated_genes.Definition: Modulates fission of macropinosomes but also affects some aspects of membrane remodeling; not essential but may crosstalk with CLIC/GEEC.

inhibitors.Definition: Sterol derivative (membrane lipid packing disruptor), dose:Effective at 10-20 µM

inhibitors.name: Methyl-beta-cyclodextrin
inhibitors.Definition: Cholesterol-depleting agent, dose:Typically 1-10 mM for 30 min

inhibitors.name: Nystatin

pathways.definition: This rapid, dynamin-dependent route is non-constitutive and specifically triggered by ligand binding to cargo receptors, such as G-protein-coupled receptors and receptor tyrosine kinases, using endophilin clusters to drive vesicle formation within ten seconds

pathways.definition: This dynamin-dependent CIE pathway is specifically regulated by the small GTPase RhoA to internalize the beta-chain of the interleukin-2 receptor and other proteins that partition into detergent-resistant membrane domains

associated_genes.name: RHOA
associated_genes.Definition: RhoA regulates actomyosin contractility and has been shown to inhibit clathrin-mediated endocytosis; activated RhoA delays EGF receptor trafficking to lysosomes.

associated_genes.name: CDC42
associated_genes.Definition: Cdc42 is essential for actin comet tail formation and vesicle propulsion in both endocytic and exocytic pathways, and regulates apical/basolateral trafficking in polarized epithelial cells.

associated_genes.name: RAC1
associated_genes.Definition: Rac1 promotes macropinocytosis and phagocytosis by driving actin polymerization through the Arp2/3 complex, but also inhibits clathrin-mediated endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: ROCK1
associated_genes.Definition: A downstream effector of RhoA, ROCK1 mediates actomyosin contractility which may influence vesicle scission or cortical tension during endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: DIAPH1
associated_genes.Definition: DIA1 (mDia1): A RhoA effector coordinating actin and microtubule networks, possibly impacting vesicle transport and membrane tension.

associated_genes.name: PKN1
associated_genes.Definition: Activated by RhoB, PRK1 is involved in trafficking of internalized EGF receptor by slowing its movement toward lysosomes.

associated_genes.name: PAK1
associated_genes.Definition: A Rac/Cdc42 target required for macropinocytosis; activated PAK1 stimulates actin remodeling linked to vesicle uptake.

associated_genes.name: SYNJ2
associated_genes.Definition: (Synaptojanin 2) Effector of Rac1 that regulates clathrin-mediated endocytosis by modifying phosphoinositides and potentially uncoating vesicles.

associated_genes.name: DNM2
associated_genes.Definition: (Dynamin 2) Interacts with cortactin and syndapin to promote membrane scission and is linked to actin-driven vesicle propulsion via Arp2/3 complex.

associated_genes.name: CTTN
associated_genes.Definition: CORTACTIN: Binds Arp2/3 and actin, mediating actin polymerization at endocytic sites; interacts with dynamin.

associated_genes.name: ARHGEF2
associated_genes.Definition: GEF-H1: A RhoGEF localized to microtubules; may activate RhoA upon microtubule depolymerization and regulate spatial activation of Rho signaling.

associated_genes.name: WASL
associated_genes.Definition: N-WASP: Activated by Cdc42 and dynamin, N-WASP drives Arp2/3-mediated actin polymerization to form vesicle-propelling actin tails.

associated_genes.name: ARPC2
associated_genes.Definition: (part of Arp2/3 complex) Effector of Rac and Cdc42 via WASP/WAVE, it nucleates branched actin networks crucial for vesicle formation and movement.

associated_genes.name: IQGAP1
associated_genes.Definition: Cdc42-interacting scaffold protein localized at Golgi; may help organize actin, but dispensable for vesicle internalization.

pathways.definition: This dynamin-independent route is regulated by CDC42 and its interaction with the actin-polymerization machinery to internalize fluid-phase markers and GPI-anchored proteins through large surface invaginations

associated_genes.name: CDC42
associated_genes.Definition: Central small GTPase that directly regulates actin polymerization via WASptoArp2/3 to drive clathrin-independent endocytosis and vesicle scission.

associated_genes.name: WAS
associated_genes.Definition: WASp: Effector of Cdc42; relieves autoinhibition and activates the Arp2/3 complex, enabling branched actin formation required for vesicle scission.

associated_genes.name: ACTR2
associated_genes.Definition: Arp2: Actin nucleator downstream of Cdc42toWASp; required to generate branched actin filaments that deform membranes and support vesicle budding and fission.

associated_genes.name: ACTR3
associated_genes.Definition: Arp3: Actin nucleator downstream of Cdc42toWASp; required to generate branched actin filaments that deform membranes and support vesicle budding and fission.

associated_genes.name: DNM1
associated_genes.Definition: Required for vesicle scission at adherens junctions and other sites where Cdc42 regulates endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: TRIP10
associated_genes.Definition: Cip4: Cdc42-interacting F-BAR protein that activates WASp and promotes Arp2/3-mediated actin assembly, facilitating vesicle scission.

associated_genes.name: PARD6A
associated_genes.Definition: Forms a complex with Cdc42 and aPKC to regulate endocytosis and recycling of clathrin-dependent and independent cargo, especially in epithelial polarity contexts.

associated_genes.name: PRKCI
associated_genes.Definition: aPKC: Kinase activated by Cdc42toPar6; regulates apical endocytosis and recycling, influencing trafficking of Crumbs and adherens junction components.

associated_genes.name: PARD3
associated_genes.Definition: Par3: In some contexts (C. elegans, Drosophila embryo) cooperates with Cdc42toPar6toaPKC to regulate apical endocytosis and recycling of clathrin-independent cargo.

associated_genes.name: CRB1
associated_genes.Definition: Crumbs: An apical determinant regulated by Cdc42; its trafficking defects destabilize adherens junctions in Drosophila neuroectoderm, but Cdc42 endocytosis can still occur without it.

associated_genes.name: CDHR1
associated_genes.Definition: DE-cadherin: Core adhesion molecule that accumulates abnormally when Cdc42/Par complex is disrupted; influences junctional stability but is not required to initiate Cdc42-mediated uptake.

associated_genes.name: CDHR2
associated_genes.Definition: DE-cadherin: Core adhesion molecule that accumulates abnormally when Cdc42/Par complex is disrupted; influences junctional stability but is not required to initiate Cdc42-mediated uptake.

associated_genes.name: CDHR3
associated_genes.Definition: DE-cadherin: Core adhesion molecule that accumulates abnormally when Cdc42/Par complex is disrupted; influences junctional stability but is not required to initiate Cdc42-mediated uptake.

associated_genes.name: CDHR4

associated_genes.name: CDH23
associated_genes.Definition: DE-cadherin: Core adhesion molecule that accumulates abnormally when Cdc42/Par complex is disrupted; influences junctional stability but is not required to initiate Cdc42-mediated uptake.

associated_genes.name: LLGL1
associated_genes.Definition: Lgl (Lethal giant larvae): Can interact with Par6toaPKC to exclude Par3; modulates polarity and trafficking in certain contexts but is not universally required for Cdc42-driven endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: LLGL2
associated_genes.Definition: Lgl (Lethal giant larvae): Can interact with Par6toaPKC to exclude Par3; modulates polarity and trafficking in certain contexts but is not universally required for Cdc42-driven endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: ANXA2
associated_genes.Definition: Annexin 2: Recruits Cdc42 to PIP2-rich apical membranes in MDCK cysts, helping lumen formation; enhances spatial targeting but is not essential for the mechanism itself.

associated_genes.name: WASF1
associated_genes.Definition: SCAR/WAVE: Alternative actin nucleation regulator that works with Cip4; contributes to endosome dynamics in Drosophila but is not indispensable for Cdc42-mediated endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: WASF2
associated_genes.Definition: SCAR/WAVE: Alternative actin nucleation regulator that works with Cip4; contributes to endosome dynamics in Drosophila but is not indispensable for Cdc42-mediated endocytosis.

associated_genes.name: WASF3
associated_genes.Definition: SCAR/WAVE: Alternative actin nucleation regulator that works with Cip4; contributes to endosome dynamics in Drosophila but is not indispensable for Cdc42-mediated endocytosis.

inhibitors.Definition: Acts as a negative regulator of Cdc42→Par6→aPKC at adherens junctions; its loss increases endocytosis and disrupts apical tension, while Cdc42 depletion rescues Rho1 mutant phenotypes.

inhibitors.name: QRICH1
inhibitors.Definition: A GAP that inactivates Cdc42 at tight junctions; its knockdown leads to Cdc42 hyperactivation and junctional defects, indicating it restrains excessive Cdc42-driven endocytosis.

pathways.definition: This clathrin-independent pathway uses the small GTPase ARF6 to regulate the internalization and recycling of membrane proteins involved in cell adhesion and immune response, such as integrins and MHC-I, through the modulation of actin cytoskeleton reorganization

associated_genes.name: ARF6
associated_genes.Definition: Master small GTPase required for clathrin-dependent and clathrin-independent endocytosis, regulating receptor and ligand uptake.

associated_genes.name: PIP5K1B
associated_genes.Definition: Generates PI(4,5)P2 at the plasma membrane after activation by ARF6, essential for coated pit assembly and scission.

associated_genes.name: PLD1
associated_genes.Definition: Produces phosphatidic acid, which cooperates with PIP5K to promote vesicle budding and fission.

associated_genes.name: PLD2
associated_genes.Definition: Produces phosphatidic acid, which cooperates with PIP5K to promote vesicle budding and fission.

associated_genes.name: PSD

associated_genes.name: CYTH2

associated_genes.name: IQSEC1
associated_genes.Definition: BRAG2 (ARF GEP100): ARF6-specific GEF required for integrin endocytosis and recycling, crucial for pathway activation in adhesion contexts.

associated_genes.name: NME1
associated_genes.Definition: NM23-H1: Supplies local GTP for dynamin during vesicle fission, enhances clathrin-mediated uptake but pathway can still occur without it.

associated_genes.name: SMAP1
associated_genes.Definition: ARF6 GAP that interacts with clathrin heavy chain to regulate vesicle uncoating; modulates efficiency but not initiation.

associated_genes.name: ACAP1
associated_genes.Definition: ARF6 GAP that regulates transferrin and beta1 integrin recycling; affects recycling but not initial uptake.

associated_genes.name: EXOC4
associated_genes.Definition: Exocyst complex (Sec10, Arfophilin-1/2): Tethers ARF6-derived recycling vesicles to plasma membrane and cleavage furrow; important for trafficking but not required for endocytosis initiation.

associated_genes.name: EXOC5
associated_genes.Definition: Exocyst complex (Sec10, Arfophilin-1/2): Tethers ARF6-derived recycling vesicles to plasma membrane and cleavage furrow; important for trafficking but not required for endocytosis initiation.

associated_genes.name: EXOC8
associated_genes.Definition: Exocyst complex (Sec10, Arfophilin-1/2): Tethers ARF6-derived recycling vesicles to plasma membrane and cleavage furrow; important for trafficking but not required for endocytosis initiation.

associated_genes.name: RAC1
associated_genes.Definition: Small GTPase activated downstream of ARF6 to remodel actin; influences endocytosis efficiency and cell migration but not strictly required for uptake.
